channel.title: Benchmark PS Blog
channel.description: Practical insights on physiotherapy outcomes, MSK measurement, and evidence-based practice.

item.title: Why Physiotherapy Clinics That Measure Outcomes Retain More Patients (and Charge More)
item.description: Data shows clinics using objective outcome measurement retain 73% more patients and command premium rates. Here's why measurement drives both clinical and commercial success.

item.title: Single-Leg Calf Raise: What the Test Tells You About Achilles and Calf Capacity
item.description: The single-leg calf raise test reveals critical insights about Achilles tendon and calf capacity. Learn evidence-based testing protocols for clinical practice.

item.title: How to Implement Objective Physiotherapy Assessment Without Expensive Equipment
item.description: Practical guide to implementing objective physiotherapy assessment using software-enabled, low-tech tools. Evidence-based protocols for standardised measurement.

item.title: MSK Outcome Benchmarks: What Good Conservative Care Actually Looks Like
item.description: Evidence-based MSK outcome benchmarks reveal what effective conservative care achieves. Learn the measurable standards that separate good physiotherapy from guesswork.

item.title: Evidence-Based Progression in Physiotherapy: A Framework for Time-Pressured Clinicians
item.description: Evidence-based progression frameworks for time-pressured physiotherapists. Practical criteria, objective markers, and clinical decision-making tools.

item.title: Why 43% of Physiotherapy Care Is Non-Recommended — and What Clinics Can Do About It
item.description: 43% of physiotherapy care doesn't follow evidence-based guidelines. Learn why treatment variation occurs and how standardised measurement reduces clinical uncertainty.

item.title: Knee Extension Strength After ACL Reconstruction: Quadriceps Force and Return-to-Sport Criteria
item.description: Evidence-based protocols for measuring quadriceps strength recovery after ACL reconstruction. Clinical benchmarks for safe return-to-sport decisions.

item.title: ACL Return-to-Sport Testing: Which Criteria Actually Predict Re-Injury Risk
item.description: Evidence-based return-to-sport criteria after ACL reconstruction. Which tests actually predict re-injury risk and which are just tradition?

item.title: The State of Technology in Physiotherapy: What Works, What Doesn't, and What's Coming
item.description: Evidence-based analysis of physiotherapy technology adoption, effectiveness, and future directions. From telerehabilitation to mobile apps - what works in practice.

item.title: Hip Strength Testing in Athletes: The Adductor-Abductor Balance That Predicts Groin Injury
item.description: Hip adductor-abductor strength ratios predict groin injuries in athletes. Learn evidence-based testing protocols and normative benchmarks for clinical practice.

item.title: Knee Osteoarthritis and Conservative Care: Setting Realistic Expectations With Objective Data
item.description: Evidence-based expectations for knee osteoarthritis treatment. How objective measurement helps clinicians set realistic goals and improve patient outcomes.

item.title: Shoulder Rotation Strength in Overhead Athletes: The ER/IR Ratio That Changes Management
item.description: Evidence-based external rotation to internal rotation strength ratios for overhead athletes. Critical benchmarks that guide return-to-sport decisions.

item.title: Trunk Endurance Testing in Low Back Rehabilitation: What Plank, Side Plank, and Sorenson Tell You
item.description: Master trunk endurance testing with plank, side plank, and Sorenson protocols. Evidence-based normative values and clinical applications for low back pain.

item.title: The Economic Case for Better MSK Conservative Care
item.description: How standardised measurement and consistent conservative care protocols reduce costs, improve outcomes, and make MSK services more defensible for commissioners.

item.title: The Business Case for Outcome Measurement in Physiotherapy Clinics
item.description: Why outcome measurement isn't just good clinical practice—it's essential for defensible, profitable physiotherapy. Evidence-based business case for UK clinics.
